# restclient-jackson-default-impl

Jackson defaultImpl oneOf sample

- API version: 0.0.1

- Generator version: 7.24.0-SNAPSHOT

Sample spec exercising Jackson @JsonTypeInfo(defaultImpl = ...) on oneOf interfaces, driven by the x-jackson-default-impl vendor extension (spec-based) and the typeInfoDefaultImpls generator option (config-based).



*Automatically generated by the [OpenAPI Generator](https://openapi-generator.tech)*

## Requirements

Building the API client library requires:

1. Java 17+
2. Maven/Gradle

## Installation

To install the API client library to your local Maven repository, simply execute:

```shell
mvn clean install
```

To deploy it to a remote Maven repository instead, configure the settings of the repository and execute:

```shell
mvn clean deploy
```

Refer to the [OSSRH Guide](http://central.sonatype.org/pages/ossrh-guide.html) for more information.

### Maven users

Add this dependency to your project's POM:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.openapitools</groupId>
  <artifactId>restclient-jackson-default-impl</artifactId>
  <version>0.0.1</version>
  <scope>compile</scope>
</dependency>
```

### Gradle users

Add this dependency to your project's build file:

```groovy
  repositories {
    mavenCentral()     // Needed if the 'restclient-jackson-default-impl' jar has been published to maven central.
    mavenLocal()       // Needed if the 'restclient-jackson-default-impl' jar has been published to the local maven repo.
  }

  dependencies {
     implementation "org.openapitools:restclient-jackson-default-impl:0.0.1"
  }
```

### Others

At first generate the JAR by executing:

```shell
mvn clean package
```

Then manually install the following JARs:

- `target/restclient-jackson-default-impl-0.0.1.jar`
- `target/lib/*.jar`

## Getting Started

Please follow the [installation](#installation) instruction and execute the following Java code:

```java

import org.openapitools.client.*;
import org.openapitools.client.auth.*;
import org.openapitools.client.model.*;
import org.openapitools.client.api.DefaultApi;

public class DefaultApiExample {

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        ApiClient defaultClient = new ApiClient();
        defaultClient.setBasePath("http://localhost:8080");
        
        DefaultApi apiInstance = new DefaultApi(defaultClient);
        Animal animal = new Animal(); // Animal | 
        try {
            Animal result = apiInstance.createAnimal(animal);
            System.out.println(result);
        } catch (HttpStatusCodeException e) {
            System.err.println("Exception when calling DefaultApi#createAnimal");
            System.err.println("Status code: " + e.getStatusCode().value());
            System.err.println("Reason: " + e.getResponseBodyAsString());
            System.err.println("Response headers: " + e.getResponseHeaders());
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    }
}

```

## Documentation for Authorization

## Recommendation

It's recommended to create an instance of `ApiClient` per thread in a multithreaded environment to avoid any potential issues.
